questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the average of the four bar values shown: $4, 6, 8, 10$.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='90' width='25' height='30'/><rect x='85' y='75' width='25' height='45'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='45' width='25' height='75'/></g><text x='55' y='138' font-size='8'>4, 6, 8, 10</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A triangle is divided by a single line from its apex to the midpoint of its base (figure). Count the total number of triangles in the figure.

figure.caption: Triangle with one median
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,120 160,120 100,30' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='100' y1='30' x2='100' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><text x='35' y='140' font-size='8'>count the triangles</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count small and combined triangles.

**Working.** Two small right triangles, plus the whole large triangle, give $2+1=3$.

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the word most similar in meaning to 'ABATE'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Abate' means to become less intense or to reduce.

**Working.** Among the options, 'diminish' is the closest synonym; 'increase' is its antonym.

**Answer.** diminish.

questions.stem: Compute the volume of the cube of side $5$ shown.

figure.caption: Cube
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='60' width='70' height='70' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><polygon points='40,60 70,30 140,30 110,60' fill='#93c5fd' stroke='#475569'/><polygon points='110,60 140,30 140,100 110,130' fill='#60a5fa' stroke='#475569'/><text x='30' y='155' font-size='8'>side 5</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An article costing $200$ is sold for $250$. The profit percentage is _____ % .

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A circle is inscribed in a square of side $10$ (figure). Compute the shaded area outside the circle but inside the square (use $\pi=3.1416$, two decimals).

figure.caption: Inscribed circle in a square
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='25' y='25' width='120' height='120' fill='#2563eb'/><circle cx='85' cy='85' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb'/><text x='30' y='168' font-size='8'>square 10, inscribed circle</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Shaded $=$ square area $-$ circle area, with $r=5$.

**Working.**
$$A=10^2-3.1416(5)^2=100-78.54=21.46$$

**Answer.** $21.46$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many triangles are there in the square with one diagonal drawn (shown)?

figure.caption: A square with a single diagonal, splitting it into two triangles.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 130 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='15' y='15' width='100' height='100' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='15' y1='15' x2='115' y2='115' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** One diagonal cuts the square into two equal triangles.

**Working.** Each half is a triangle, so there are $2$.

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Pipe A fills a tank in $6$ hours and pipe B in $12$ hours. With both open, the tank fills in _____ hours.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the filling rates.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{6}+\dfrac{1}{12}=\dfrac{2+1}{12}=\dfrac{3}{12}=\dfrac{1}{4}$, so $4$ hours.

**Answer.** $4$ hours.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one interior angle is $70^\circ$ (see figure), its co-interior (same-side) angle is

figure.caption: Two parallel lines crossed by a transversal; one interior angle is marked 70 degrees.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='60' x2='200' y2='60' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='20' y1='120' x2='200' y2='120' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='60' y1='30' x2='160' y2='150' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><text x='95' y='78' font-size='11' fill='#ef4444'>70</text><text x='118' y='112' font-size='11' fill='#10b981'>?</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Co-interior (same-side interior) angles between parallel lines are supplementary.

**Working.** $180^\circ-70^\circ=110^\circ$.

**Answer.** $110^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Notice: 'During a fire, use the stairs; do not use the lift.' The assumption underlying this notice is that
options: lifts may malfunction or be unsafe during a fire
there is never a real fire
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An assumption is what must be taken for granted for the advice to make sense.

**Working.** Advising against lifts during a fire presumes lifts are unsafe then.

**Answer.** lifts may malfunction or be unsafe during a fire.
